summary: 2026-07-17 11:53 PDT — The same-filesystem atomic-promotion fix is correct, but the cleanup trap has a narrow pre-installation leak window and the drift test covers only one of the two local temp templates.

findings.title: Cleanup trap is installed after both allocations
findings.claim: The trap is registered only after both `mktemp` calls. If the shell exits or is interrupted after the first temp is created but before line 67 executes—including failure of the second allocation under `set -e`—the first unpromoted temp remains, contrary to the stated cleanup guarantee.
findings.suggestion: Initialize both variables and register the EXIT trap before the first `mktemp`, then assign each temp path.

findings.title: Drift check covers only the correctness temp
findings.claim: The new check pins the reviews-local template for `tmp_c` only. Moving `tmp_h` back to a system temp would still pass this test, despite the story's fix record saying the tests pin both local temps.
findings.suggestion: Add a presence check for `mktemp reviews/.<slug>.hidden-failure.XXXXXX`.
